---
title: "DS4420 Final Project: Bayesian Model"
author: "Zoe Garbus & Evelyn Robert"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-16"
---
## Bayesian Classification: Traffic Accident Severity
### Predicts severity (1-4) from environmental/situational features

### Feature Engineering

```{r}
library(brms)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

# load cleaned data
accidents <- as.data.frame(read_csv("accidents_clean.csv"))

# feature engineering
# bin hour into time of day
accidents$TimeOfDay <- cut(accidents$Hour,
                           breaks = c(-1, 5, 11, 16, 20, 23),
                           labels = c("Night", "Morning", "Afternoon", "Evening", "LateNight"))

# simplify weather into broad buckets
accidents$Weather_Bucket <- ifelse(grepl("Rain|Drizzle|Shower|Thunder|T-Storm", accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Rain",
                            ifelse(grepl("Snow|Sleet|Hail|Ice|Wintry", accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Snow",
                            ifelse(grepl("Fog|Mist|Haze|Smoke", accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Fog",
                            ifelse(grepl("Cloud|Overcast", accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Cloudy",
                            ifelse(grepl("Clear|Fair|Sunny",accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Clear", "Other")))))

# brms needs categorical variables as factors, not integers
accidents$IsNight <- as.factor(accidents$Sunrise_Sunset == "Night")
accidents$Severity <- as.factor(accidents$Severity)
accidents$Weather_Bucket <- as.factor(accidents$Weather_Bucket)
accidents$TimeOfDay <- as.factor(accidents$TimeOfDay)

# scale continuous features
accidents$Temp <- scale(accidents$`Temperature(F)`)
accidents$Humidity <- scale(accidents$`Humidity(%)`)
accidents$Visibility <- scale(accidents$`Visibility(mi)`)
accidents$Distance <- scale(accidents$`Distance(mi)`)

# remove other since it doesn't end up being included in test sample
accidents <- accidents[accidents$Weather_Bucket != "Other", ]
accidents$Weather_Bucket <- droplevels(accidents$Weather_Bucket)

# downsample to balance classes (severity 2 and 3 dominate)
set.seed(42)
balanced <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  s <- accidents[accidents$Severity == k, ]
  target <- case_when(
    # keep severity 1 & 4 as is, downsample 2 & 3 so it evens out dataset
    # for mdoeling purposes
    k == 1 ~ 81,
    k == 2 ~ 800,
    k == 3 ~ 800, 
    k == 4 ~ 207
  )
  s[sample(nrow(s), min(nrow(s), target)), ]
}))

# train/test split (80/20)
train_idx <- sample(nrow(balanced), floor(0.8 * nrow(balanced)))
train <- balanced[train_idx, ]
test  <- balanced[-train_idx, ]

print(table(balanced$Severity))
```
### Fitting the model

```{r}
# check default priors
default_prior(Severity ~ Temp + Humidity + Visibility + Distance + IsNight + Weather_Bucket + TimeOfDay,
              data = train,
              family = categorical("logit"))

# define manual priors
manual_prior <- c(
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu2"),
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu3"),
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu4"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu2"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu3"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu4")
)

# fit the model
severity_brm <- brm(
  Severity ~ Temp + Humidity + Visibility + Distance + IsNight + Weather_Bucket + TimeOfDay,
  family = categorical("logit"),
  data = train,
  chains = 4,
  iter = 2000,
  warmup = 500,
  thin = 2,
  cores = getOption("mc.cores", 4),
  prior = manual_prior
)

# diagnostics
summary(severity_brm)
plot(severity_brm)
```
### Printing out metrics

```{r}
library(caret)

# posterior predictive for one accident
# each row is an MCMC sample, each column a test observation
# values are predicted severity class labels
post_preds <- posterior_predict(severity_brm, newdata=test)

# accuracy on test set
pred_classes <- apply(post_preds, 2, function(x) as.integer(names(which.max(table(x)))))
cat("Accuracy:", round(mean(pred_classes == as.integer(test$Severity)), 4), "\n")

# confusion matrix
print(table(Predicted=pred_classes, Actual=as.integer(test$Severity)))

# matrix for precision, accuracy, and f1 scores
cm <- confusionMatrix(
  factor(pred_classes, levels = 1:4),
  factor(as.integer(test$Severity), levels = 1:4)
)

# extract and print per-class metrics
metrics <- data.frame(
  Class = paste("Severity", 1:4),
  Precision = round(cm$byClass[, "Precision"], 3),
  Recall = round(cm$byClass[, "Recall"], 3),
  F1 = round(cm$byClass[, "F1"], 3)
)

print(metrics)
```

### Plotting

```{r}
# conditional effects - plot all to see the effects
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Temp", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Visibility", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "IsNight", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Weather_Bucket", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "TimeOfDay", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Distance", categorical=TRUE))

# posterior predictive distribution for one observation
hist(post_preds[, 1], main="Post Pred Distribution: Accident 1",
     xlab="Predicted Severity", col="steelblue", border="white")
cat("True severity:", as.integer(test$Severity[1]), "\n")
```
### More plotting - to see predictor effects on accident severity 

```{r}
library(tidybayes)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

severity_brm %>%
  gather_draws(`b_.*`, regex = TRUE) %>%
  filter(.variable != "b_Intercept") %>%
  mutate(
    # extract severity class from parameter name
    severity_class = case_when(
      str_detect(.variable, "mu2") ~ "Severity 2",
      str_detect(.variable, "mu3") ~ "Severity 3",
      str_detect(.variable, "mu4") ~ "Severity 4"
    ),
    # clean up predictor names for display
    predictor = .variable %>%
      str_remove("b_mu[234]_") %>%
      str_replace_all("Weather_Bucket", "Weather: ") %>%
      str_replace_all("TimeOfDay", "Time: ") %>%
      str_replace_all("IsNightTRUE", "Is Night") %>%
      str_replace_all("Temp", "Temperature") %>%
      str_replace_all("Visibility", "Visibility") %>%
      str_replace_all("Humidity", "Humidity") %>%
      str_replace_all("Distance", "Distance")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .value, y = reorder(predictor, .value), color = severity_class, fill = severity_class)) +
  stat_halfeye(
    .width = c(0.5, 0.95),
    alpha = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Severity 2" = "#4dac26", "Severity 3" = "#d6604d", "Severity 4" = "#8B008B")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Severity 2" = "#4dac26", "Severity 3" = "#d6604d", "Severity 4" = "#8B008B")) +
  facet_wrap(~ severity_class, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Predictor effects on accident severity",
    subtitle = "Posterior distributions with 50% and 95% credible intervals. Reference: Severity 1.",
    x = "Log-odds coefficient",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Effects to the right of zero increase probability of that severity class relative to Severity 1."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )
```
